calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Mar-26-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Robert Paul Garcia for and with Barbara Davis.

Mr. Garcia addressed the issues set forth in the Examiner's notes stating that there was a discrepancy in the amount that his client owed the estate, that the correct amount is $1,911.43, that the amount of $1,767.31 was a typographical error, that the loveseat was purchased with estate funds in the amount of $520.80, that the loveseat is in Ms. Davis' possession, that the loveseat could not fit in a car to be transported, and that its transport would require the use of estate funds or they'd have to find a donation site that would pick it up. In addition, Mr. Garcia stated that the time payments regarding the assistants are set forth in the supplemental declaration, that cleaning out the condominium was a team effort, that two of the helpers were family members, and that there were a total of eight people that helped Ms. Davis. Ms. Davis stated that two of her relatives worked for two days only, that the broker's hired a person, that the broker's person did nothing/only the bottom area floor, that Mr. Pedroncini was a hoarder, that there were 30 boxes that needed to be gone through, and that all of the boxes had to be gone through carefully because she was told that she couldn't throw the documents out. Mr. Garcia stated that the property stayed unrepaired and unimproved for thirty years after the death of Mr. Pedroncini's Mother. Ms. Davis stated that she still has the loveseat, that she hasn't used it, that she had some of her things in Mr. Pedroncini's place, that she paid for the staging, that she wanted to remember her friend of 50 years, that she gave more than she ever got, that she paid for the funeral, and that she only receives her Social Security income. Mr. Garcia addressed the expense to Ms. Shields stating that there were two $1,500,000.00 claims filed against Mr. Pedroncini, that she had to reach out to the only person with knowledge - Gayle Shields, that there was 12 page report prepared, that the work product in the report helped them fight the two claims, and that Ms. Shields was asking for $1,200.00 for her report. Ms. Davis stated that she authorized $375.00 if that is okay with the Court. Mr. Garcia addressed the issue with the timesheet stating that the timesheet needed to be pasted together to correct it. The Court addressed fees. Mr. Garcia stated that they submitted a declaration addressing the fees, that Ms. Davis was asking for 72.50 hours at $65.00 an hour, and that the extraordinary compensation total that they are asking for is $4,712.00. The Court addressed the 97 hours for ordinary services at $45.00 an hour. Brief discussions are held. At the conclusion of discussions, the Court took the matter under submission.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (2987 Mission Street, Unit 404, San Francisco, Ca 94110)
Off Calendar Jul-31-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Robert Garcia for Petitioner

The sale was confirmed at $635,000.00 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (subject to review of the order). Bond is waived. Petitioner's request for 6% sales commission to brokers is DENIED.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Shirley Le. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

***Post Script***
The proceeds from the sale are to be deposited into a blocked account in lieu of bond.
Status on filing of receipt for the blocked account is set for August 22, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Probate Examiner to notify Counsel of status date and to provide the court with a revised proposed order with multiple financial institutions for deposit of blocked funds if over FDIC insured amount.
